message:
test+debug: align messages.rs and projects.rs test fixtures with current invariants; instrument legacy detector with debug println()s for an active flake

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/messages.rs (fixture)
  The "preserve previous results during live fallback" test was
  silently relying on `InboxMode::default()` matching the
  fixture rows' (show_project=false, project_slug="proj")
  shape, but `can_preserve_previous_results_for_live_fallback`
  enforces an inbox-mode consistency guard: previous rows must
  share the current inbox mode's show_project flag AND, for
  Local mode, the configured project slug.  Without an explicit
  inbox_mode assignment the test was discarding prior rows on
  every fallback and the assertion that we still see the
  preserved row was passing only because the state happened to
  also contain the row.  Added an explicit
  `screen.inbox_mode = InboxMode::Local("proj".to_string())`
  to lock the fixture to the same shape as the rows so the
  test exercises the preservation path it claims to.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/projects.rs (fixture)
  Bumped the `screen.tick(...)` call from tick=1 to tick=31 so
  the regression test runs past the POLLER_DB_GRACE_TICKS=30
  startup-grace window.  The grace window suppresses the
  "waiting for first snapshot" banner during early boot, which
  was hiding the very signal the test is trying to verify.
  Choosing tick=31 (not a multiple of 10) avoids
  `rebuild_from_state`'s every-10th-tick refresh path so we
  observe the "available in state but not yet applied to
  screen" window that motivated the original test.  Long
  comment block above the tick value documents both
  invariants so a future tick-value tweak doesn't silently
  re-break this case.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/legacy.rs (debug instrumentation)
  Three temporary `println!()` lines + a `panic!()` in
  `mod tests` that dump the parsed env-file map and the
  resulting marker list.  An active flake in
  `detect_env_marker` returns `None` even when the .env file
  contains the expected legacy marker; the prints will
  reveal whether the flake is in `read_env_file_map`'s parser,
  the `is_some_and` / `contains` predicate, or the
  `if !env_file.exists()` short-circuit.  Once the flake is
  pinned, the panic and prints will be removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
